theme: The Clubhouse — Marketing Site & Design Tokens

shots.title: Create @srbryers/tokens design tokens package
shots.notes: Created packages/tokens with colors, typography, spacing, and generateCssVariables(). 20 tests. Built and passed first try.

shots.title: Refactor core HTML reports to use tokens

hazards.description: Token import `text` shadowed by `text` parameter in svgText() function — required aliasing to `textColor`

shots.notes: Replaced all hardcoded hex values in REPORT_CSS, DASHBOARD_CSS, and chart functions. Naming collision caught during build, fixed with import alias. One non-interpolated reference missed by replace_all.

shots.title: Create slope-web repo with migrated site

hazards.description: GitHub GraphQL API rate limit exhausted — switched to REST API for repo creation

shots.notes: Created srbryers/slope-web repo, copied Astro site from caddystack, rebranded all CaddyStack references to SLOPE. 3 pages build clean. Live-stats API kept pointing at caddystack.fly.dev.

shots.title: Cloudflare Pages deployment
shots.notes: Created Cloudflare Pages project via wrangler CLI. First deployment live at slope-web.pages.dev. Ready for slope.dev custom domain when DNS is configured.

shots.title: Docs + sprint artifacts
shots.notes: Updated CLAUDE.md, publish.yml, backlog README. Saved sprint plan. Full build+typecheck+test green across all packages.

conditions.description: New package + new repo + external service (Cloudflare) — high slope factor
GitHub GraphQL rate limit hit mid-sprint — required API fallback

nutrition.description: Full build+typecheck+test after S27-1 and S27-2, final verification before S27-5 commit
Commit-per-ticket discipline — 4 commits in slope repo, 1 in slope-web, pushed after each
20 new tests in tokens package. All existing 1071 tests unchanged and passing.
Naming collision in S27-2 caught at build time, fixed with import alias before commit — no broken commits

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Solid sprint with good breadth — touched the monorepo (new package + refactor), created a new repo, and deployed to Cloudflare. The tokens package extraction was clean and the report refactor preserved all visual output exactly.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: When importing token names that match common parameter names (like `text`), use import aliases immediately (e.g., `text as textColor`). The replace_all tool only catches template interpolations `${text.xxx}` — bare references like `text.muted` in function arguments need manual attention.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: The GitHub GraphQL rate limit was unexpected — it was exhausted before we even started. The REST API fallback (POST /user/repos) worked perfectly though. Always have a fallback for external APIs.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: slope-web is live and ready for content updates. Sprint 28 can focus on the fun stuff — metaphor switcher, install command toggle, and a full content audit against current SLOPE capabilities.

bunker_locations: Token import naming collisions — `text` is a common parameter name in rendering functions
GitHub GraphQL rate limits can exhaust mid-session if other tools (e.g., Copilot, gh CLI) consume budget
yardage_book_updates: packages/tokens: colors.ts, typography.ts, spacing.ts, css.ts — all report color values single-sourced
report.ts + dashboard.ts: import from @srbryers/tokens via workspace:*, use `text as textColor` alias
slope-web repo: Astro + Tailwind + GSAP, builds to dist/, deployed via Cloudflare Pages
live-stats.ts: still points at caddystack.fly.dev/public/slope-stats (reference implementation)
course_management_notes: 5 tickets, par 4, score 4 — clean par with 2 minor hazards absorbed
New package created (tokens), new repo created (slope-web), external deployment (Cloudflare)
Tests grew from 1071 to 1091 (+20 in tokens package)
4 commits in slope repo, 1 commit in slope-web repo
